116748.explanation: ITH considers it extremely important that our residents have an emergency brake as a counterbalance to potentially wrong decisions. A referendum is the most democratic way to decide on a topic. ITH believes every referendum request from residents should be honored.

116751.explanation: When a farmer wants to operate a shop or campsite, it is important to look together with neighbors whether this is desirable and appropriate at that location. Tailored solutions are the principle. ITH gladly supports initiatives that contribute to local food production.

116752.explanation: ITH strives for 100% local ownership in sustainable energy generation. ITH believes the municipality has a facilitating and stimulating role in generating sustainable energy. That can, but does not always have to, include financial support.

116754.explanation: ITH is not in favor of large-scale asylum seeker reception of this kind. Essenkade lacks a solid business case and sufficient guarantees for safety and liveability. ITH wants to work with residents to find other solutions to comply with the distribution law, such as housing more status holders.

116757.explanation: Open fireplaces and wood stoves sometimes pose mild health risks. ITH believes residents should have their own freedom of choice in this. However, we do support education and strict requirements for new stoves and open fireplaces.

116758.explanation: Hospitality entrepreneurs should be given the opportunity to welcome customers on their terraces outside the season by using terrace heating. This contributes to a good business climate and ensures a livelier and more pleasant Houten, even outside the season.

116761.explanation: ITH wants to continue encouraging events that contribute to a more vibrant and sociable Houten by allowing room for new ideas and making these as easy to implement as possible. Clear communication and support regarding noise standards are naturally important.

116763.explanation: Due to the housing shortage, ITH finds it important that all possibilities for additional housing are seriously considered. The existing housing space in Houten should be better utilized. It is important that there is good resident participation and support. Additionally, any plan must fit within Houten's DNA.

116765.explanation: ITH considers a local broadcaster crucial because it keeps residents informed about local developments. We currently see no reason for additional funding, as the municipality already provides subsidies. We consider tailored solutions appropriate if necessary, given the importance of a local broadcaster.

116768.explanation: Investing in sports is investing in people and health. ITH has recently facilitated financial contributions to keep sports clubs open, such as the Startsprong swimming pool. We have also supported the petition to keep the outdoor swimming pool open.

116770.explanation: More enforcement is essential for safety, residents' sense of security, and livability. Increased enforcement can contribute, among other things, to safer cycling traffic, reduced common crimes, less nuisance (near shopping centers), and a safer feeling for women at night.

116771.explanation: ITH is a strong advocate for camera surveillance at neighborhood entrances with hotspots, near shopping centers, and at events (such as fairs). The use of cameras has a preventive effect, increases the chance of catching offenders, and can strengthen residents' sense of security.

116776.explanation: ITH wants to maximize the use of the existing housing stock by shared housing, adding extra floors, and splitting homes. Also, using holiday homes as permanent residences is an opportunity to quickly create extra housing for starters and young people since it does not require new construction.

116777.explanation: There is an enormous housing shortage in Houten, so all options must be utilized, including building beyond existing village boundaries, otherwise the shortage will persist for many years. The area vision for Houten-East (for 2,750 homes) has already been approved and positively received by residents.

116779.explanation: Every resident must pay municipal taxes, but tailored payment arrangements should be offered to residents with low incomes to prevent them from falling into (further) debt.

116780.explanation: The municipality must focus on preventing poverty. A higher municipal income threshold could give more people access to support agencies but may also lead to higher financial burdens for the municipality. Therefore, ITH supports tailored solutions rather than a generic increase.

116784.explanation: For ITH, the starting point is that the quality of care must be adequate, waiting lists reduced, and care provided close to residents. This is a priority for ITH, and given the scarcity of caregivers, absolute freedom of choice is unrealistic.

116786.explanation: ITH does not want further tax increases for our residents due to the large price increases in recent years. Given the severe housing shortage, increasing this residential tax is a very unwise choice for ITH.

116788.explanation: ITH opposes increasing the tourist tax in Houten because it is already considerably higher than in municipalities in the region. Houten currently ranks 9th in the list of highest tourist taxes. This is detrimental to the competitive position of entrepreneurs in Houten.

116789.explanation: ITH wants business activity to be concentrated as much as possible at the Doornkade business park and other suitable business areas. This contributes to revitalization of these areas and creates an attractive climate for new and existing companies.

116791.explanation: In principle, ITH does not favor giving priority to status holders in housing allocation. Given the severe housing shortage, it should not be automatic that status holders take precedence over Houten residents in allocations.

116793.explanation: ITH does not want a third wind farm. Where possible, sustainable energy should be promoted and applied as much as possible. Therefore, ITH wants to focus on large-scale use of solar energy in the coming period. Good examples are the Knoest and the Heul solar parks on former agricultural land.

116794.explanation: The Theater Aan de Slinger has earned its own place in Houten society. ITH has invested extra in art, culture, and the library for years. We want to maintain this level and prevent cuts in this sector.

116803.explanation: If parking nuisance is observed in certain neighborhoods, options for more parking spaces must be investigated. ITH favors sufficient parking spaces, both in existing and new neighborhoods, for Houten residents.

116805.explanation: ITH does not want new wind turbines in Houten. The Energy Citizens’ Council showed that there is no support for more wind farms. Wind turbines placed so close to residential areas damage the living environment of residents. For this reason, ITH has always opposed the Goyerbrug wind farm.

116806.explanation: A 'Houten museum' can be of cultural historical value to Houten society. Research has already been done on such a museum, and ITH supports this initiative because it contributes to heritage education.

116807.explanation: On busy shared paths, we want to increase safety by better regulating traffic and addressing the nuisance of fast bicycles such as fat bikes and speed pedelecs. ITH advocates a maximum speed limit of 20 km/h for cyclists.

116748.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA favors good participation at an early stage so that residents can influence important issues. In some situations, we believe it should also be possible for residents to express their opinions via an advisory referendum with clear questions and choices.

116751.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes small-scale recreation should be possible in the rural area. Selling sustainable and local products can also help farmers secure a sustainable future.

116752.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that residents can contribute to the energy transition themselves. The municipality can support them by pointing out subsidy opportunities and by financially supporting local energy cooperatives.

116754.explanation: For GroenLinks-PvdA, it is important that our municipality shows solidarity with other municipalities and shelters refugees in a humane and safe manner, in a way that fits Houten society.

116757.explanation: Wood smoke is harmful to public health and air quality, especially for people with respiratory issues, particularly in unfavorable weather conditions. GroenLinks-PvdA therefore wants Houten to develop discouragement policies and promote adherence to the RIVM's burning guide.

116758.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA prefers to discourage terrace heating. This type of heating is energy-intensive and causes additional CO2 emissions. This does not fit a sustainable municipality like Houten. A blanket is just as cozy and much more sustainable.

116761.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes festivals and parties should be possible within reasonable limits. A good balance is needed between recreational enjoyment and the burden on local residents and nature. Proper enforcement is important.

116763.explanation: This is already arranged in Houten. Farmers can convert their farmyards and barns into small-scale residential locations. We support preserving the open character of the rural areas to ensure enough green space remains.

116765.explanation: A local broadcaster is important for community spirit and local democracy. The current funding is sufficient to maintain a well-functioning local broadcaster. According to GroenLinks-PvdA, no extra money is needed now.

116768.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes sports clubs should be financially self-sufficient. The municipality supports sports clubs so that everyone has ample opportunity to play sports. This can be through providing good facilities and sports fields and by making them more sustainable.

116770.explanation: There are enough public space officers (boa’s), but sometimes recruitment is difficult. GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that existing boa’s are better deployed to enhance (the feeling of) safety. Usually, with existing boa’s, safety can be adequately maintained.

116771.explanation: Currently, camera surveillance is used well and effectively. It does not automatically lead to more safety but does infringe on privacy. GroenLinks-PvdA believes privacy must be safeguarded.

116776.explanation: This issue does not apply in Houten.

116777.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA aims to preserve the special character of the rural area but sees opportunities for small-scale building projects at the village edges ('borders'). Within the ring road, there is enough space for housing without building in Houten-East.

116779.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA's principle is that everyone should be able to build a decent life with sufficient income security. The strongest shoulders must bear the heaviest burdens. That is fair.

116780.explanation: The municipality must do more here. GroenLinks-PvdA supports raising the standard from 120% to 130% of the social assistance norm.

116784.explanation: This is not a municipal issue but a national one. A caregiver must primarily be good and reliable.

116786.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important to maintain general facilities. In times of upcoming cuts, increasing the property tax (ozb) may be necessary.

116788.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important to keep the theater, swimming pool, and similar facilities open. We do not want to cut back here and will therefore need to increase our income. Tourist tax can contribute to this and enable investments in green, small-scale recreation.

116789.explanation: Houten has a good mix of living and working. GroenLinks-PvdA believes that the existing business parks offer sufficient opportunities for entrepreneurs to settle. Existing business parks can be renewed and made more sustainable.

116791.explanation: Refugees should be able to start integrating as soon as possible. A home is part of that, as well as learning the language and working. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA considers it important that refugees with a residence permit remain one of the groups given priority for rental housing.

116793.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA clearly prefers solar panels on roofs, verges, parking lots, and the like, and only as a very last resort on agricultural land. To achieve our goal of making Houten energy neutral by 2040, we do not want to exclude solar fields on agricultural land either.

116794.explanation: The theater is highly valued in Houten and attracts many visitors. GroenLinks-PvdA believes it should continue to receive sufficient support from the municipality. It is important that the theater has the space to sustain itself financially.

116803.explanation: There are currently enough parking spaces in Houten. GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that the municipality stimulates public transport and car sharing in new construction projects, so that more space can be made available for green and affordable housing.

116805.explanation: It is important that Houten is energy neutral by 2040 and thus contributes to a clean and safe future. With the current plans, we will not make it. GroenLinks-PvdA wants to consult with residents to see how this can be made feasible. New wind turbines are certainly an option.

116806.explanation: The history of Houten and its beautiful heritage are very valuable to GroenLinks-PvdA, but above all, we want to make better use of existing cultural infrastructure, such as the Archaeological Working Group, to tell the story of Houten.

116807.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds this necessary for the safety of various users on the cycle paths. Young and old must be able to move safely in Houten. This is especially necessary for fast mopeds, fatbikes, and partly also for electric bicycles.

116748.explanation: Our residents already have the opportunity to initiate a referendum. D66 Houten believes this right must be preserved. The council must take the outcome of the referendum into account in its decision.

116751.explanation: D66 Houten supports local entrepreneurship and farmers’ initiatives. Each application is evaluated based on nature, safety, and spatial planning. This way, the living environment remains good and attractive for everyone.

116752.explanation: Generating energy together encourages sustainability, innovation, and local involvement. The municipality supports initiatives that make Houten greener and energy-independent but does not finance them.

116754.explanation: Every municipality must arrange shelter. A reception center with good guidance helps people start safely and contributes to a warm, involved society. Integration and work start on day one and proceed in consultation with all involved parties.

116757.explanation: D66 Houten gives people the freedom to decide for themselves whether they want to use an open fireplace. Traditional fire is cozy but can also be harmful to health and the environment. We seek a balance between atmosphere and sustainable air quality for everyone.

116758.explanation: Terrace heating adds to comfort but increases energy consumption and CO₂ emissions. We encourage smart, sustainable alternatives for outdoor heating.

116761.explanation: D66 Houten believes that this kind of event makes Houten lively. Current noise standards sufficiently consider preventing nuisance to nearby residents.

116763.explanation: Farmers have submitted good housing initiatives in recent years. Each initiative is examined for its impact on nature, traffic, livability, and spatial planning. As far as D66 Houten is concerned, this will not change.

116765.explanation: Local media connect residents, bring news and culture close, and strengthen involvement. Support helps maintain a strong, independent broadcaster.

116768.explanation: Sports clubs are social and connecting. Temporary support helps them stay healthy and accessible to everyone.

116770.explanation: Safety is important but not only through more enforcement officers. Prevention, smart enforcement, and cooperation create a safe municipality.

116771.explanation: Camera surveillance can help, but privacy and livability are crucial. D66 Houten seeks a balance between safety and the freedom of residents.

116776.explanation: D66 Houten sees no reason to change the existing rules. We will focus mainly on building more affordable housing in the coming years.

116777.explanation: D66 Houten wants space for housing but also to protect nature in the rural area. If there are plans to build outside the village boundaries and this is good for the livability and quality of the village, D66 Houten supports this.

116779.explanation: Those with little should be spared. Therefore, the option for debt relief must remain. It helps people make ends meet and prevents financial problems from worsening.

116780.explanation: This threshold was recently adjusted so that more people can receive help from the municipality. D66 Houten sees no reason to raise the limit further now.

116784.explanation: Care must be personal and respectful. Freedom of choice in care increases trust and quality for everyone.

116786.explanation: Taxes must remain fair and affordable. D66 Houten wants to protect our social base (such as sports, culture, and welfare), and therefore, an increase is unfortunately necessary.

116788.explanation: Tourists contribute to Houten, but a tax increase can discourage visitors. We seek a balance between revenue and attractiveness.

116789.explanation: The economy is important, but expansion must be sustainable, smart, and fit with the living environment and mobility. D66 Houten prefers new construction within existing parks.

116791.explanation: D66 Houten wants this for all urgent seekers! So also for our own residents who immediately need another home.

116793.explanation: Solar energy is important, but agricultural land is scarce. We seek smart combinations of energy generation and food production.

116794.explanation: Culture connects and enriches Houten. Investing in theater stimulates creativity, participation, and social life.

116803.explanation: As a cycling municipality, we especially want to promote bicycle use. D66 Houten mainly wants more attention for greenery and playgrounds in neighborhoods to beautify our municipality.

116805.explanation: Sustainable energy is necessary. We aim for a balance between energy, nature, and living environment. A possible new location must be carefully chosen. Therefore, research is first needed into two areas to map possibilities and obstacles.

116806.explanation: History connects residents and makes Houten proud. As far as D66 Houten is concerned, there is no need for a separate building; the museum can have a place in, for example, the library, the theater, or the town hall.

116807.explanation: Safety is important, but overly strict speed limits can be inconvenient. We seek smart measures for safe cycling in busy areas.

116748.explanation: When it comes to an important municipality-wide issue (as was the case with the Spatial Course), but not every topic is suitable for this.

116751.explanation: Provided it is small-scale, we are positive about this in the context of entrepreneurship by farms.

116752.explanation: The municipality can provide support for the startup of a project by residents (financially in exceptional cases), but the sustainable energy project must pay for itself.

116754.explanation: Under the Distribution Act, we are obliged to accommodate asylum seekers and want to do so in a good, humane way, with proper conditions regarding safety, target group, numbers, location, etc. This should clarify the best form in which this can be done.

116757.explanation: We do not want to ban it completely, but we do want to discourage its use, especially during moments when there is no wind.

116758.explanation: We are not strong supporters of heating outdoor air and prefer alternatives (such as blankets), but we do not believe the municipality should enforce regulations on this.

116761.explanation: We support festivals and parties (such as a third half) for community bonding, but there must be no hearing damage to visitors, and nuisance to surrounding neighborhoods must be considered.

116763.explanation: Given the housing shortage, it would be nice if multiple generations could live on one property, and perhaps a Knarrenhof (a senior cohousing community) is also possible.

116768.explanation: Sports clubs are important for social interaction and health. Support is justified when they are in trouble, but always as a consideration. Clubs also bear responsibility for their own income; municipal support is supplementary and aimed at continuity and accessibility.

116770.explanation: This can be combined with neighborhood connectors. Law enforcement officers (BOAs) can be especially deployed for street safety at night.

116771.explanation: We are not against camera surveillance, provided it is used flexibly for a limited period. Extension is possible if the situation calls for it.

116776.explanation: There are very few holiday homes in the municipality of Houten.

116777.explanation: We want to build a beautiful, green, sustainable neighborhood of the future in Houten-East, which all of Houten can be proud of!

116779.explanation: Debt problems can be persistent and the municipality must be able to provide tailored solutions.

116780.explanation: Debt problems can be persistent and the municipality must be able to provide tailored solutions, but this does not necessarily have to be through raising the income threshold to receive aid.

116784.explanation: Bundling care is important to provide quality care, so this is not always possible. But if it can, we want to pay attention to it.

116786.explanation: Only if necessary to maintain facilities. We do not want to have to close the swimming pool.

116789.explanation: We can better utilize and renovate existing industrial areas.

116791.explanation: There must be a proportionate balance between refugees and local housing seekers from Houten. Every municipality has a quota for status holders, so Houten must also contribute. However, the wait time for housing seekers from Houten is currently too long (about 10 years).

116793.explanation: Only if there is dual use of space, such as solar panels above fruit trees or vertical solar panels.

116794.explanation: We are proud that Houten has a theater and want to keep it that way. We would like to explore additional financial sources such as new sponsors, including a paid naming sponsor for the theater.

116803.explanation: We want to preserve space for greenery, playgrounds, and meeting places.

116805.explanation: Houten has already made a significant contribution with two wind parks. Together with the region, we will explore what further is possible and necessary.

116806.explanation: We already address archaeology at the Oude Station, and that can possibly be expanded.

116807.explanation: For safety on bicycle paths, it is important to reduce the speed of fatbikes, scooters, and speed pedelecs.

116748.explanation: We are against referenda; in our view, they reduce deliberations to a simple yes/no. This can lead to impulsive choices without consideration of long-term consequences. We believe complex political decisions are better made by elected representatives.

116751.explanation: We believe there must always be room for more entrepreneurship. However, there are conditions that it can only exist if it does not unreasonably infringe on the freedoms of others.

116752.explanation: We are against structural financial support. Sustainable initiatives are valuable but should primarily arise from their own strength and through the market. The municipality facilitates where needed, without extra burdens for all residents.

116754.explanation: Every municipality must take responsibility. We only allow an asylum seekers center (azc) under strict conditions regarding numbers, livability and safety for the location and surroundings, and financial feasibility for the municipality. We do not want to house unaccompanied minor asylum seekers.

116757.explanation: We are against banning open fireplaces and wood stoves. Residents should retain freedom of choice in their own homes. Responsible use is important to limit nuisance and health risks; education can help with this.

116758.explanation: We are against banning terrace heaters. Entrepreneurs should have space to operate their business profitably. We prefer to encourage sustainability with tailored solutions and innovation rather than bans.

116761.explanation: We are against stricter noise standards. Events contribute to liveliness and community bonding in the municipality. With clear agreements and enforcement, nuisance can be limited without additional rules.

116763.explanation: We support this possibility for company residences. Additionally, we want to allow experiments creating one or more hamlets if there is a good plan for it. These hamlets can strengthen the rural area by increasing the number of available homes.

116765.explanation: We are against extra subsidies for the local broadcaster. The municipality must handle taxpayers' money carefully and be cautious with structural subsidies in addition to existing support.

116768.explanation: Sports clubs are primarily responsible for themselves. The municipality can think along and facilitate but must be cautious in using public funds.

116770.explanation: More enforcement officers can ensure that people adhere better to the rules, so Houten remains beautiful and livable.

116771.explanation: We are in favor of more camera surveillance. It can contribute to safety, prevention, and crime detection. With clear rules and attention to privacy, it is an effective supplementary tool.

116776.explanation: Not applicable: there are no holiday homes in Houten.

116777.explanation: To address the housing shortage, expansion is needed, also beyond existing boundaries. In Houten-East, we see room for a new neighborhood, with attention to accessibility, greenery, and livability.

116779.explanation: We support ending the waiver of municipal taxes, because structural tax waivers are income politics. We want municipal funds to be spent frugally and fairly.

116780.explanation: We are against raising the income threshold. Too generous schemes increase the risk of a poverty trap: people barely benefit from working more. Aid must remain focused on those who really need it.

116784.explanation: Freedom of choice is important, but care must remain accessible, affordable, and of good quality for everyone. Mandatory provision of specific care based on religion or culture is not feasible.

116786.explanation: We are against raising property tax (ozb). Housing costs and expenses for entrepreneurs must remain affordable. The municipality must first critically review its spending before raising taxes.

116788.explanation: We are against raising the tourist tax. It harms Houten’s appeal to visitors and entrepreneurs. The municipality should be cautious about increasing charges. The tourist tax in Houten is already very high compared to other municipalities.

116789.explanation: We are in favor of expanding industrial areas. This is important for employment and a strong local economy. However, available space is limited, so expansion requires careful decisions.

116791.explanation: We are against continuing to give priority to refugees with a residence permit for social rental housing. The waiting lists in Houten are already long. Priority does not do justice to other housing seekers and is not fair.

116793.explanation: We are against more solar panels on agricultural land. Fertile soil is scarce and needed for food production. Solar panels preferably belong on roofs and other already built-up locations.

116794.explanation: We are against giving more money to Theater Aan de Slinger. The municipality must handle taxpayers' money carefully and be cautious about further increasing subsidies.

116803.explanation: We are neither for nor against more parking spaces in the neighborhoods. Whether expansion is needed depends on the actual demand per neighborhood. Tailored solutions and livability are paramount. If more spaces are needed, it is fine to add them.

116805.explanation: We do not want additional wind turbines after the installation near the Goyerbrug. The impact on local residents, landscape, and living environment is significant. Compared to other municipalities, Houten has already generated very much sustainable energy.

116806.explanation: Our history is important to learn from. That can also be done by creating the canon of Houten with 50 key events as a basis for a virtual museum or by making them visible in the library, socio-cultural centers, and town hall, with QR codes and without high costs.

116807.explanation: We support a maximum speed of 20 km/h on bicycle paths. This increases safety and prevents dangerous situations, especially for children and the elderly, in a busy cycling municipality like Houten.

116748.explanation: Residents can already express their opinions via a referendum. We continue to support this, as it is now.

116751.explanation: This is a very good way to keep the countryside and farms healthy and attractive.

116752.explanation: We welcome cooperatives of residents working together to generate (large-scale) sustainable energy. We should support this.

116754.explanation: Houten must comply with the distribution law and must also feel solidarity with other municipalities that also accommodate asylum seekers. You can no longer ignore this. Houten must take its responsibility too.

116757.explanation: For existing fireplaces and wood stoves, we do not want to prohibit them by default but follow the guidelines of the Stookwijzer. For new houses, the possibility of having a fireplace or wood stove is already discouraged.

116758.explanation: It is important that people can still sit on a terrace when it is a bit cooler. That makes the Rond and the Oude Dorp cozy.

116761.explanation: Young people should also be able to find a fun and pleasant place to go out in Houten.

116763.explanation: This can help farmers who want to stop find a good new income model.

116765.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives a subsidy and does not need extra money.

116768.explanation: Sports clubs primarily support themselves but we will not let them fail.

116770.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) are visible and close to the people. It is important that attention is given to this.

116771.explanation: We agree that in some situations extra camera surveillance is justified but want to prevent many additional cameras. We believe that increases the feeling of insecurity.

116776.explanation: Houten has no holiday parks, so that is not an issue here.

116777.explanation: We want building to start quickly in Houten-Oost, and small-scale building outside the red contours is also allowed near the small villages. There is a large housing need.

116779.explanation: It is essential for some residents to make ends meet. We do not want to change that.

116780.explanation: Houten already applies a high income limit compared to neighboring municipalities.

116784.explanation: If this can be taken into account here, that is good. But we see care as a different matter compared to education, for example. In care, that choice must be available.

116786.explanation: If that is necessary, then it is allowed. But we want to avoid it as much as possible.

116788.explanation: Hotel Houten has been allowed to expand. It is therefore not surprising that the Houten community can benefit from this through increased tourist tax.

116789.explanation: We primarily focus on better utilizing current business parks instead of expanding. We prefer to dedicate valuable land to housing. But if it is economically necessary, small-scale expansions of business parks must remain possible.

116791.explanation: Yes, this is a legal requirement.

116793.explanation: We have always supported this. That is why 100 hectares of solar fields have already been permitted. For now, it may stay there and especially other municipalities in the region must increase large-scale sustainable generation.

116794.explanation: The theater already receives a subsidy. That should continue. We will not let the theater fail, but it is also important that it generates sufficient income itself.

116803.explanation: We find most locations fine now. If parking problems arise, we must see if we can solve them. Possibly, in some situations, additional parking spaces are needed. We want to provide more room for car sharing.

116805.explanation: We have supported both wind farms. We do not think more wind farms should be added. Possibly, near the highway at the sewage treatment plant, a solitary wind turbine could be added aligned with the other wind turbines.

116806.explanation: If there are initiators for this, we want to support it further. But it is not a must for us.

116807.explanation: Fatbikes and scooters cause a lot of nuisance. As a cycling village, we think the speed should be lowered for faster users. However, it will likely be very difficult to enforce this.

116748.explanation: A consultative referendum is possible and must remain possible in Houten, with the final decision resting with the municipal council.

116751.explanation: This is an important way for farmers to remain profitable. The shop or campsite must fit in the environment.

116752.explanation: Except in the form of regular and accessible subsidies for everyone.

116754.explanation: Houten must accommodate refugees according to the law. Moreover, SGP-Houten feels a calling to help neighbors in need. We do this in a Houten way: distributed, together with the environment and the broader community, and by setting clear conditions.

116757.explanation: Using a fireplace or wood stove is a personal choice for residents, but they can seek advice from the Stookwijzer.

116758.explanation: Terrace heating is a way for local entrepreneurs to be hospitable. With sustainability measures, entrepreneurs can consider reducing unnecessary use of terrace heating.

116761.explanation: Especially in the rural areas of Houten, noise standards for festivals and parties must be stricter due to nuisance.

116763.explanation: Only if this fits within the current regulations.

116765.explanation: Local and critical journalism is very important, but SGP-Houten considers the existing subsidies sufficient.

116768.explanation: The municipality already fully supports sports, so no additional funds are needed. If there are financial difficulties, the municipality can work with the association to find solutions.

116770.explanation: According to SGP-Houten, Houten must be clean, intact, and safe; for that, community officers (boa’s) are essential.

116771.explanation: There has been too little camera surveillance for a long time, while this demonstrably works preventively.

116776.explanation: Because of the small number of holiday homes, this measure has virtually no impact.

116777.explanation: This is urgently needed for Houten-East. Elsewhere, only if appropriate and small-scale, and with strong involvement of the villages themselves.

116779.explanation: This is already regulated at present; SGP-Houten wants to keep it that way.

116780.explanation: There are currently many different schemes; there needs to be one clear policy on poverty reduction.

116786.explanation: The burden on residents must remain low. The municipality must seek ways to prevent costs from skyrocketing.

116788.explanation: This does not burden Houten residents, and tourism is not essential for Houten.

116789.explanation: There is little 'free space' left in Houten, but we do want to invest in existing industrial estates.

116791.explanation: Status holders deserve a roof over their heads, but other groups also have priority. SGP-Houten wants to focus on other solutions to meet the quota for status holders.

116793.explanation: There is no more capacity on the grid for solar panels on agricultural land. If capacity becomes available, SGP-Houten supports solar panels above fruit cultivation.

116794.explanation: Theater Aan de Slinger already receives large amounts of subsidy; no additional money is needed.

116803.explanation: If there is demonstrable need for more parking spaces, customization should be possible.

116805.explanation: There are enough large wind turbines in Houten. However, space must be made for farmers' windmills in the rural area.

116806.explanation: Houten has a long and rich history. All residents of Houten should be able to learn about this in a museum about Houten's history. Together with residents, we can come closer to this dream of SGP-Houten.

116807.explanation: Fatbikes and mopeds that go faster than other traffic pose a danger on Houten's bicycle paths. Therefore, experiments with a maximum speed on bicycle paths should be conducted.

116748.explanation: We find it especially important that residents can participate deliberately and clearly know in advance what involvement they have in the political process (informing, advising, co-deciding, etc.). Furthermore, politicians are already representatives of the people, rendering a referendum unnecessary.

116751.explanation: We support own initiatives of communities, including citizens and farmers.

116752.explanation: This promotes the energy transition and puts control in the hands of the residents themselves.

116754.explanation: We find hospitality important: it is a Biblical mandate not to look away from people in need. At the same time, a good safety plan, involvement of local residents, and serious consideration of concerns are important. This also applies to integration into society.

116757.explanation: In an emergency, alternative heating is necessary. We find that self-reliance outweighs the environmental impact.

116758.explanation: The use of terrace heaters in the hospitality sector uses unnecessarily much energy that is needed elsewhere.

116761.explanation: A party is fun, but it must remain a fun party for residents nearby as well. Residents currently experience noise nuisance at events, so that requires attention.

116763.explanation: We agree with this statement, provided it means additional living space for family or informal care.

116765.explanation: Free media, including local broadcasters, form an important part of democracy. Moreover, they have a positive function in emergencies.

116768.explanation: Disagree, because an association primarily has its own board and responsibility. Moreover, sports preferences change, so interest in a particular sport can disappear. Then a new association should be given a chance.

116770.explanation: Safety is an important factor in a developing place. We want to counteract unwanted gatherings and promote traffic safety and safety for women.

116771.explanation: Only camera surveillance where it has proven added value.

116776.explanation: This should only be allowed in emergencies, such as in case of divorce. Deliberate residence by, for example, labor migrants should be discouraged. The municipality must assess whether there is an emergency situation that warrants tolerance.

116777.explanation: The term 'village boundary' is too vague in this statement. Furthermore, it is questionable what influence the municipality has regarding housing construction outside the village boundary.

116779.explanation: Especially in such situations, the municipality is the safety net for people who fall through the cracks. Abuse cannot be completely ruled out, but it is too limited to spend much energy on. Solidarity clearly comes to the fore here.

116780.explanation: The statement is somewhat unclear. Wherever we can combat poverty, we try to do so. This reduces the pressure of financial worries and allows people to focus on what truly matters. Letting the income threshold grow at least with inflation can help, provided it remains demonstrably effective.

116784.explanation: In the Netherlands, freedom of choice and freedom of religion are great values that we must cherish. Especially in healthcare, it is important that people feel seen from their background and faith.

116786.explanation: We choose 'agree', although reluctantly. This depends on the upcoming budget, but with foresight we think this unfortunately seems necessary to properly realize other plans, such as healthcare.

116788.explanation: This can be increased with the inflation correction.

116789.explanation: Where this is possible and desirable, and old sites are transformed into housing.

116791.explanation: This is desirable to promote integration of newcomers who are allowed to stay for a longer time and to relieve the flow-through in Ter Apel. However, it should be considered whether this priority percentage should be slightly lowered for a good balance compared to other home seekers.

116793.explanation: The municipality should first fully utilize the roofs of industrial estates, and secondarily the roofs of houses. If all roofs are covered, then we really do not need that agricultural land.

116794.explanation: Unfortunately, there is already a large deficit in the municipal budget now, so this is not a wise choice.

116803.explanation: This comes at the expense of housing construction and living space, which are urgently needed.

116805.explanation: Wind turbines are necessary for the energy transition. However, their placement should be well integrated into the landscape and should, as much as possible, avoid open sightlines.

116806.explanation: This seems like a good plan to us because it puts Houten into perspective and adds value. We see that there is a broad group of volunteers and a historical association active who want to commit themselves to this. We support these resident initiatives.

116807.explanation: We do wonder how this will be enforced. Furthermore, existing future plans speak of 25 km/h instead of 20 km/h. We see no reason to suddenly lower it slightly.

116748.explanation: For major and impactful issues, residents must be able to express their opinions. A referendum can help with this, provided it is well prepared and residents are clearly informed.

116751.explanation: Not everything can be allowed everywhere. Initiatives must fit with the landscape, traffic, and livability. Therefore, we choose tailor-made solutions instead of automatic approval.

116752.explanation: The municipality simply cannot finance the entire energy transition. Such initiatives should, however, be given plenty of space and the municipality should be facilitating.

116754.explanation: Houten takes responsibility, but only with local control, clear agreements, and involvement of local residents. Humane shelter and public support must go hand in hand. This means that solutions other than a reception center are welcome, but so far we have seen no realistic alternatives.

116757.explanation: Health and air quality are important. Restrictions may be necessary in case of nuisance or poor air quality. Houten has co-signed the Clean Air Agreement and from there education about nuisance caused by wood burning could be a task. But a total ban goes too far.

116761.explanation: Events contribute to liveliness but should not cause structural nuisance. We seek balance between enjoyment and livability.

116763.explanation: This can contribute to housing construction, but only if it is spatially and scenically well integrated. No blank approval.

116765.explanation: A strong local broadcaster strengthens democracy and involvement. Local news provision is essential for a well-informed community. However, the local broadcaster has become a regional broadcaster, and all participating municipalities must contribute to this.

116768.explanation: Associations are the social heart of Houten. Yet they must stand on their own feet. The municipality should not turn a blind eye if an association is in trouble and must see what it can do to help. The energy subsidy during the energy crisis was a good example of this.

116770.explanation: A feeling of safety is important and currently enforcers and police are rarely visible, especially in the evenings.

116771.explanation: Houten must have camera surveillance, not necessarily more but used smartly. So not always in the same place, but moving between hotspots and places with many reports of criminal behavior.

116776.explanation: This is not a structural solution to the housing shortage and can displace recreation.

116777.explanation: In principle, building within the red contours as established by the Province. However, sometimes expansion is necessary, and there must be a possibility to build outside them. For example, see Houten-East.

116779.explanation: Exemption from payment protects people with low incomes. We want to retain that possibility.

116780.explanation: Too many people are currently just outside the arrangements. Expanding them prevents problematic debts.

116784.explanation: People have the right to choose their own care.

116786.explanation: As with all financial measures, there are no sacred cows. Preferably we do not increase property tax, but it must remain possible to discuss it as a measure.

116788.explanation: That could be defensible if the proceeds also directly benefit the tourist and recreational facilities.

116789.explanation: Business activity is important. What we would like is for Doornkade to get a quality boost and to look at which business activities fit better inside the ring road and which outside it.

116791.explanation: The municipality could abandon this, but other municipalities have shown that this is not necessarily a good solution and contributes little to shortening waiting times.

116793.explanation: First, the solar field policy must be evaluated and the need for more solar panels on agricultural land must be inventoried. Only after new frameworks are set can new solar fields be considered.

116794.explanation: The theater fulfills an important cultural and social function in Houten. However, the theater will also need to show entrepreneurship so that it becomes less dependent on municipal subsidies.

116803.explanation: We seek a balance between parking, greenery, and living space.

116805.explanation: The municipality must first draw up the wind framework we requested. Without frameworks, you cannot make good choices. With a wind framework, you can reasonably oppose additional wind turbines. And also support them, by the way.

116806.explanation: Currently, it is being considered how that could possibly find a place in the new House of Houten.

116807.explanation: First, Houten must develop policy regarding the use of bike paths at different speeds. Enforcing a speed limit is, by the way, difficult.

116748.explanation: We support participation but not referendums. Referendums make complex choices black-and-white and increase polarization. Therefore, Volt prefers citizens' assemblies, where randomly selected residents study issues and jointly develop supported solutions. Citizens' assemblies lead to better decisions and more connection.

116751.explanation: The municipality can give farmers space for side activities such as a farm shop or small-scale camping, provided they fit the landscape, environment, and traffic safety. With clear frameworks and tailor-made solutions, we strengthen the rural area without harming livability.

116752.explanation: Volt believes the energy transition should be by and for residents. Local energy cooperatives provide support, affordability, and ownership in the neighborhood. Municipal support – for example through loans, guarantees, or start-up subsidies – helps to remove barriers.

116754.explanation: Houten must take responsibility in refugee shelter. We do this humanely and with dignity: small scale, well organized, and with attention to livability, safety, and support. With good guidance and opportunities to participate, for refugees and the neighborhood.

116757.explanation: Volt strongly focuses in its program on a healthy living environment, sustainability, and an energy-neutral municipality. Open fires hardly fit with this due to negative effects on air quality and health.

116758.explanation: Volt supports discouraging terrace heating and offering blankets as a sustainable alternative.

116761.explanation: Festivals and parties belong to Houten and make the municipality lively. Too strict noise standards unnecessarily complicate organizing them, but that does not mean that everything should be allowed. With clear agreements and enforcement, we maintain the balance between enjoyment and livability.

116763.explanation: Small-scale housing construction should be possible under strict conditions and with clear public objectives, especially when a farmer ceases agricultural activities.

116765.explanation: A strong local broadcaster provides reliable information close to home. It connects residents and strengthens local democracy. Independent and accessible reporting is important. This way, local news remains by and for the people of Houten.

116768.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for health and social interaction in Houten. If they encounter difficulties, the municipality should help. This does not always have to be financial: support can also involve guidance, relieving burdens, or smarter regulation of rules and housing.

116770.explanation: Crime in Houten has increased and there are many reports of youth nuisance. Additional community officers can help better map disturbances and work together with youth, police, and partners on structural solutions and increased safety.

116771.explanation: The privacy of every individual is a great asset, and therefore Volt is cautious and advocates very careful, transparent, and proportionate use. In Houten, Volt focuses on prevention, good lighting, a neighborhood-oriented approach, and the presence of enforcement.

116776.explanation: The municipality should not simply allow holiday homes as permanent residences. Before this can happen, the impact on the local environment and facilities to and from the holiday homes must first be considered.

116777.explanation: The housing shortage calls for realism. Building only within existing boundaries is insufficient. By building carefully and nature-inclusive also beyond village borders, we keep Houten livable and offer prospects to young people, families, and the elderly.

116779.explanation: Debt forgiveness prevents small amounts from turning into large debts and provides peace and clarity. Problem debts also cost the government an enormous amount of money. Therefore, we want to actively tackle problematic debts, for example by buying them and paying them off.

116780.explanation: Many people earn just too much for assistance but still struggle to make ends meet. This causes stress and uncertainty. By raising the income limit, we can also help this group in time and prevent future costs.

116784.explanation: Volt believes good care should be equally accessible to everyone and that patients' backgrounds must be taken into account. However, 'always being able to choose' is not realistic and makes care unnecessarily expensive. Care providers and patients must therefore treat each other with respect.

116786.explanation: We want to maintain a vital and pleasant municipality. This requires sufficient resources for care, safety, greenery, and good facilities in the neighborhoods. Therefore, we choose to invest in the future, the wellbeing of Houten residents, and a financially healthy administration.

116788.explanation: We find it reasonable that visitors share in the costs of the places they enjoy. The tourist tax helps keep Houten livable, maintain good facilities, and keep culture thriving.

116789.explanation: In Houten, business parks can expand moderately if necessary for local employment and a sustainable economy, with strict requirements for land use, accessibility, energy, and landscape integration. No large-scale growth without clear added value for Houten.

116791.explanation: The municipality should continue to prioritize refugees with residence permits for social rental housing. This ensures rapid integration, clarity, and prevents prolonged stays in shelters, while we simultaneously continue to invest in sufficient affordable housing for everyone.

116793.explanation: Solar energy is indispensable. Solar panels on agricultural land are possible, but only under strict conditions. We first utilize roofs and existing infrastructure. Solar parks are only relevant with multiple use, local ownership, and careful landscape integration.

116794.explanation: Art and culture are essential for a vital municipality. Theater Aan de Slinger deserves stable and future-proof funding. Additional financing is possible if linked to clear objectives: accessibility for all residents, space for local talent, and a broad cultural offering.

116803.explanation: The municipality opts for livable neighborhoods and smart use of space. Instead of automatically adding more parking spaces, we focus on shared cars, good cycling facilities, and smarter use of existing spaces, so public areas remain green and accessible.

116805.explanation: Houten must contribute to combating climate change and meeting energy goals. Wind energy can play a role, but not without limits. After the park near the Goyer Bridge, we critically assess necessity, integration, with attention to landscape, livability, and support.

116806.explanation: There is already a museum located in the old station. Additionally, the new cultural center can provide space to make the history of Houten visible, for example through (temporary) exhibitions and public activities.

116807.explanation: A maximum speed of 20 km/h on bike paths within the built-up area can contribute to safety and predictability in Houten, especially where many different users come together. Tailored solutions are needed: enforcement and design must suit the type of bike path and usage.

statements.title: Houten must get a museum about the village's history.
In Houten the use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves must remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In Houten moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The residents of Houten must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Houten moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Houten must have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Houten must increase camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: A maximum speed of 20 km/h must apply on the bike paths in Houten.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The business parks in Houten may expand.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: There should be more parking spaces in the neighborhoods.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Houten should give Theater Aan de Slinger more money.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should oppose installing any more wind turbines after the second wind farm near the Goyerbrug.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zich verzetten tegen het plaatsen van meer

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

titleParts.text: na het tweede windmolenpark bij de Goyerbrug.
